conversation.content:
Reconcile the following columns. Document has 11 pages. Sources are anonymous (A and B).

COLUMNS:

---
Column 1: Median OS (mo) | Overall | Treatment
Definition: What is the median overall survival (in months) for all patients in the experimental arm? If reported, include the value; use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="NE" | page=6 | modality=figure | reasoning="Figure 1 shows 'Median Survival (95% CI) mo Darolutamide NE'. NE stands for Not Estimable, which implies not reached."
  B: value="NE" | page=6 | modality=figure | reasoning="From Figure 1, 'Overall Survival (Full Analysis Set)', the median survival for Darolutamide is 'NE' (Not Estimable)."

---
Column 2: Median OS (mo) | Overall | Control
Definition: What is the median overall survival (in months) for all patients in the control arm? If reported, include the value; use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="48.9" | page=6 | modality=figure | reasoning="Figure 1 shows 'Median Survival (95% CI) mo Placebo 48.9 (44.4-NE)'."
  B: value="48.9" | page=6 | modality=figure | reasoning="From Figure 1, 'Overall Survival (Full Analysis Set)', the median survival for Placebo is 48.9 months (95% CI, 44.4–NE)."

Figure 1. Overall Survival (Full Analysis Set).
Kaplan–Meier estimates of overall survival are shown. For the analysis of overall survival, data were censored as of the last known date
the patients were alive. One patient who was randomly assigned to the placebo group but received darolutamide was included in the
placebo group in the full analysis set. CI denotes confidence interval, and NE not estimable.



SECONDARY EFFICACY END POINTS
Darolutamide was associated with significantly
greater benefits than placebo for the first five sec-
ondary efficacy end points tested hierarchically



(Table 2 and Fig. 2). The time to development of
castration-resistant disease was significantly lon-
ger in the darolutamide group (hazard ratio, 0.36;
95% CI, 0.30 to 0.42; P<0.001). The time to pain
